excluded_defs.name: toJson
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand how it delegates to Utils::jsonEncode with ignoreErrors=true, but not modified

excluded_defs.name: jsonEncode
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand return value on failure ('null' string), but not modified

exploration_log.aha_moment: toJson is called with $ignoreErrors=true, causing Utils::jsonEncode to return the string 'null' on failure with no way to tell if encoding failed or data was literally null. json_last_error() retains the last error even after the suppressed @json_encode call, so checking it immediately after toJson reveals the failure.
exploration_log.hindsight: Going straight to JsonFormatter::format() was correct. The Utils::jsonEncode path is clean but I needed to understand it returns 'null' not false on error.

alternatives: Modify Utils::jsonEncode to return structured fallback
key_decisions.decision: Fix in JsonFormatter::format() by checking json_last_error() after toJson call
key_decisions.reasoning: Task explicitly says fix JsonFormatter.php. Modifying Utils or NormalizerFormatter would affect all callers beyond JsonFormatter.

search_sequence.result: format() calls toJson($normalized, true) — the true is ignoreErrors

search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to see what ignoreErrors does
search_sequence.result: delegates to Utils::jsonEncode which returns 'null' on failure

search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to confirm what 'null' return means and how json_last_error() behaves
search_sequence.result: Confirmed @json_encode is used when ignoreErrors=true, and json_last_error() will hold the error code after the suppressed call

queries.query_text: JsonFormatter format silent failure on json_encode error, fix with json_last_error fallback

solve_notes: Modified JsonFormatter::format() to check json_last_error() immediately after calling toJson(). When an encoding error is detected, the method saves the error code and message, then encodes a safe fallback object containing the error description and the sanitized (UTF-8 converted) original message. This ensures no silent empty output on encoding failures.
task_text:
Fix JsonFormatter encoding errors silently producing empty output

When `JsonFormatter::format()` encounters a record containing data that
fails `json_encode()` (e.g., invalid UTF-8 sequences), it returns an
empty string or `null` instead of a fallback representation. Fix
`JsonFormatter.php` to detect encoding failures via `json_last_error()`
and produce a safe fallback JSON string containing the error details
and the sanitized message.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: toJson
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: needed to understand that toJson with ignoreErrors=true returns the string 'null' on failure (not false), and that json_last_error() still holds the error after the suppressed @json_encode call

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set (format method) is what gets edited. The thrash_preventing set adds toJson because an AI agent implementing the fallback logic needs to know the exact return value on failure ('null' string, not false) and that json_last_error() is still accessible after the suppressed @json_encode inside toJson. Without reading toJson, an agent might incorrectly check `if ($json === 'null')` or `if ($json === false)` rather than checking json_last_error().
